BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Freelance rate-strategy review ahead of Ray Mitchell mentor coffee. Question: is the $75/hr rate covering all costs or leaving me short?

CurrentState.Note: Baseline built from 2026 YTD averages. Rate raise to $95/hr would shift buffer to ~$1,900.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Rate raise is the most direct lever. Client mix (5 clients across 3 tiers) is stable — the constraint is $/hr, not utilization.
ActionItems: Raise Verdant and Pinnacle retainers at renewal (Q1 2027)
Draft new rate sheet before Ray coffee (Oct 23)
Model Q4 2026 tax impact of higher revenue before raising
